benefits.title: Programme teams can evidence and claim their graduate futures value more confidently
benefits.statement: Programme teams can make stronger, more nuanced and evidence-informed claims about the futures their programme makes possible for students, including distinctive excellence, student pathways, outcomes and value.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, faculties, students, applicants, recruitment teams and King's Edge
benefits.benefitType: programme-level graduate futures evidence and articulation
benefits.successLooksLike: Programme-level narratives claim graduate futures value with confidence, nuance and evidence, while remaining context-sensitive, academically owned and appropriately caveated. These local stories are strong enough to be used by programme teams and to feed King’s-level graduate premium synthesis through 2.4.1.
evidenceOfSuccess: Programme reflections, profiles or story materials explain what programmes enable for students using evidence, context and academic judgement.
Programme teams recognise themselves in the story and feel able to use it with students, applicants, colleagues or external audiences.
Student-facing, applicant-facing or programme-facing claims about graduate futures are evidence-linked, context-sensitive, appropriately caveated and agreed with the relevant programme teams.

Programme stories provide credible local material that can feed institutional graduate premium evidence and related King’s Edge work, including 2.4.1, without reducing programme value to a single metric.

enabledBy.outputTitle: First working graduate futures evidence pack

benefits.title: Programme teams and faculties understand graduate futures enhancement opportunities better
benefits.statement: Programme teams, departments and faculties can see where existing strengths should be amplified and where curriculum, skills, opportunity, support or articulation work could most strengthen students' futures.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, faculties, Careers and Employability Service, King's Edge and students
benefits.successLooksLike: Programme and faculty conversations use graduate futures evidence to identify strengths, questions, support needs and focused opportunities for enhancement without treating the evidence as a deficit report, ranking or judgement on programme quality. These local insights are clear enough to support action and to feed King’s-level pattern-finding through 2.4.1.
evidenceOfSuccess: Guided conversations capture agreed questions, practical local actions or areas for further support.
Actions or focus areas are taken into normal programme, department or faculty planning routes rather than remaining as standalone project paperwork.
Faculty-level learning draws out recurring strengths, questions, student pathways and support needs across local contexts.

Local themes inform faculty planning, curriculum enhancement, skills-related activity, support priorities or King’s-level synthesis through 2.4.1 without creating programme comparison or league-table logic.

enabledBy.outputTitle: Examples of programme insight and story
Recommendations for related King's Edge work

measures.title: Confidence and credibility of programme graduate futures claims
measures.questionAnswered: Can programme teams assert their graduate futures value more confidently and credibly?
measures.measure: Review of programme reflections, profile materials, story examples and programme-team feedback on whether the evidence helps colleagues claim strengths and explain value with confidence, nuance, context and appropriate caveats, including whether local stories are usable in programme contexts and can feed 2.4.1.
measures.owner: Heads of Department, with support from Careers and Employability Service and Mark Peace
measures.cadence: During early programme partner review and annual synthesis
measures.baseline: Programme-level graduate futures stories and graduate premium claims are currently uneven and not consistently evidence-linked.
measures.target: Programme stories used in review, recruitment, open days, student-facing contexts or graduate premium evidence work are evidence-linked, context-sensitive and agreed with relevant programme teams, with local material suitable to inform 2.4.1.

measures.title: Quality of local graduate futures enhancement insight
measures.questionAnswered: Does the process help programme teams and faculties understand where graduate futures practice, support or articulation could be strengthened?
measures.measure: Review of guided conversation outputs, agreed questions, practical local actions, faculty-level learning and evidence that themes are interpreted responsibly and routed through normal programme, department, faculty or King’s Edge planning, including whether local insights are clear enough to feed 2.4.1.
measures.owner: Heads of Department, faculty education leads, Careers and Employability Service and Mark Peace
measures.cadence: Termly during supported conversations and annual synthesis
measures.baseline: Enhancement opportunities from programme-level graduate futures evidence are not yet consistently captured, synthesised or routed through local and institutional planning.
measures.target: Engaged programme groups and faculty-level learning identify strengths, questions, support needs and focused enhancement opportunities without reducing programmes to categories, rankings or deficit labels, with local insights suitable to inform 2.4.1.
